user:

### Input

* `<broken_theorem>`: The theorem declaration and the proof body that produced an error.
* `<error>`: The Lean compiler error.

<local_ctx>
import Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith

--
-- Define correctness for sorting algorithms
--

-- list is sorted, i.e. no element is smaller than its predecessor
inductive Sorted [LT T] : List T -> Prop
  | nil : Sorted []
  | singleton : forall {x}, Sorted [x]
  | cons : forall {a b xs}, Sorted (b :: xs) -> Not (b < a) -> Sorted (a :: b :: xs)

-- counts the elements equal to t in l
def count1 [DecidableEq T] (l : List T) (t : T) : Nat :=
  match l with
  | [] => 0
  | x :: xs => (if x = t then 1 else 0) + count1 xs t

-- l and m are permutations of each other, i.e. they contain the same amount of each value
def Permut [DecidableEq T] (l m : List T) : Prop := forall t, count1 l t = count1 m t

-- a sorting algorithm is correct iff it each output is a sorted permutation of the input
def SortCorrect [Preorder T] [DecidableEq T] [DecidableRel (LT.lt (α := T))] (sort : List T -> List T) : Prop :=
  forall l, And (Sorted (sort l)) (Permut (sort l) l)

--
-- Lemmas for correctness propositions
--

def insert_ [LT T] [DecidableRel (LT.lt (α := T))] (t : T) : List T -> List T
  | [] => [t]
  | x :: xs => if t < x then t :: x :: xs else x :: insert_ t xs

def insertionsort [LT T] [DecidableRel (LT.lt (α := T))] : List T -> List T
  | [] => []
  | x :: xs => insert_ x (insertionsort xs)

--
-- Verify insertion sort
--
</local_ctx>

<broken_theorem>
theorem (T : Type) [Preorder T] [DecidableEq T] [DecidableRel (LT.lt (α := T))] : SortCorrect (insertionsort : List T -> List T) := by
  let rec insert_sorted
      (t : T) :
      ∀ l, Sorted l → Sorted (insert_ t l) := by
        intro l hs
        cases hs with
        | nil =>
            simp [insert_]
            exact Sorted.singleton
        | singleton =>
            rename_i x
            by_cases htx : t < x
            · simp [insert_, htx]
              exact Sorted.cons Sorted.singleton (by
                intro hxt
                exact not_lt_of_ge (le_of_lt hxt) htx)
            · simp [insert_, htx]
              by_cases hxt : x < t
              · exact Sorted.cons Sorted.singleton hxt
              · exact Sorted.cons Sorted.singleton hxt
        | cons hs' hba =>
            rename_i a b xs
            by_cases hta : t < a
            · simp [insert_, hta]
              exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons hs' hba) (by
                intro hat
                exact not_lt_of_ge (le_of_lt hat) hta)
            · simp [insert_, hta]
              by_cases htb : t < b
              · simp [insert_, htb]
                exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons hs' (by
                  intro hbt
                  exact hta (lt_of_lt_of_le hbt (le_of_lt htb)))) hba
              · simp [insert_, htb]
                exact Sorted.cons (insert_sorted t (b :: xs) hs') hba
  let rec insert_permut
      (t : T) :
      ∀ l u, count1 (insert_ t l) u = count1 (t :: l) u := by
        intro l u
        cases l with
        | nil =>
            simp [insert_]
        | cons x xs =>
            simp [insert_]
            by_cases h : t < x
            · simp [h, count1]
            · simp [h, count1, insert_permut t xs u, Nat.add_left_comm, Nat.add_comm]
  intro l
  induction l with
  | nil =>
      constructor
      · simp [insertionsort]
        exact Sorted.nil
      · intro t
        simp [insertionsort, count1]
  | cons x xs ih =>
      rcases ih with ⟨hs, hp⟩
      constructor
      · simp [insertionsort]
        exact insert_sorted x (insertionsort xs) hs
      · intro t
        simp [insertionsort]
        rw [insert_permut x (insertionsort xs) t]
        simp [count1, hp t]
</broken_theorem>

response:
<reasoning>
The failures are all inside `insert_sorted`. In the singleton case after `¬ t < x`, the goal is `Sorted [x, t]`, so `Sorted.cons` needs a proof of `¬ t < x`, which is exactly `htx`; splitting on `x < t` was unnecessary and caused the type mismatch.

In the `cons` case with `¬ t < a`, after splitting on `t < b`:
- if `t < b`, then `insert_ t (a :: b :: xs) = a :: t :: b :: xs`, so we need `Sorted (t :: b :: xs)` and `¬ t < a`. The former is `Sorted.cons hs' htb`, and the latter is `hta`.
- if `¬ t < b`, then `insert_ t (a :: b :: xs) = a :: insert_ t (b :: xs)`, so we need `Sorted (insert_ t (b :: xs))` and `¬ head(insert_ t (b::xs)) < a`. Since `¬ t < b`, the inserted list starts with `b`, so `hba : ¬ b < a` is exactly the needed proof; `simpa [insert_, htb]` converts the shape.

Everything else can stay the same.
</reasoning>

<lean4_proof>
by
  let rec insert_sorted
      (t : T) :
      ∀ l, Sorted l → Sorted (insert_ t l) := by
        intro l hs
        cases hs with
        | nil =>
            simp [insert_]
            exact Sorted.singleton
        | singleton =>
            rename_i x
            by_cases htx : t < x
            · simp [insert_, htx]
              exact Sorted.cons Sorted.singleton (by
                intro hxt
                exact not_lt_of_ge (le_of_lt hxt) htx)
            · simp [insert_, htx]
              exact Sorted.cons Sorted.singleton htx
        | cons hs' hba =>
            rename_i a b xs
            by_cases hta : t < a
            · simp [insert_, hta]
              exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons hs' hba) (by
                intro hat
                exact not_lt_of_ge (le_of_lt hat) hta)
            · simp [insert_, hta]
              by_cases htb : t < b
              · simp [htb]
                exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons hs' htb) hta
              · simp [htb]
                simpa [insert_, htb] using
                  (Sorted.cons (insert_sorted t (b :: xs) hs') hba)
  let rec insert_permut
      (t : T) :
      ∀ l u, count1 (insert_ t l) u = count1 (t :: l) u := by
        intro l u
        cases l with
        | nil =>
            simp [insert_]
        | cons x xs =>
            simp [insert_]
            by_cases h : t < x
            · simp [h, count1]
            · simp [h, count1, insert_permut t xs u, Nat.add_left_comm, Nat.add_comm]
  intro l
  induction l with
  | nil =>
      constructor
      · simp [insertionsort]
        exact Sorted.nil
      · intro t
        simp [insertionsort, count1]
  | cons x xs ih =>
      rcases ih with ⟨hs, hp⟩
      constructor
      · simp [insertionsort]
        exact insert_sorted x (insertionsort xs) hs
      · intro t
        simp [insertionsort]
        rw [insert_permut x (insertionsort xs) t]
        simp [count1, hp t]
</lean4_proof>
